Tai Chi · Guide Series

Tai Chi Walking

Your 7-day bonus movement guide — a calm walking routine for balance, breathing and relaxed strength, with step-by-step instructions for every move.
7 daily routines · Step-by-step · Gentle & steady

Welcome to Tai Chi Walking

Tai Chi Walking turns an ordinary walk into a gentle, flowing practice — slow, mindful steps with soft arm movements and easy breathing.

Over seven short daily routines, you'll build balance and steady legs, ease tension through the arms, and calm the mind. It's low-impact, needs no equipment, and moves at your pace. Each exercise comes with a clear, step-by-step guide — start with Day 1, then repeat the week as often as you like.

How to use this guide

  • Set the space first — a clear area with room to step and turn, free of loose rugs.
  • Keep support close — a sturdy chair or wall within reach for the balance moves.
  • Choose control over range — shorter steps count; smooth and steady beats fast or forced.
  • Let the waist lead — in Tai Chi the gentle turn of the waist moves the arms, not the other way around.
  • Breathe easily — keep your breath slow and natural; never hold it.
A quick note. Move only as far as feels comfortable and steady. If you feel dizzy or unsteady, pause and rest. If you've had falls, or have a heart or balance condition, check with your doctor before starting.
1

Begin with Ease

About 11 minutes · Open space or chair nearby · 3 slow rounds
Focus: Walk smaller than you think you need.  Move through the exercises below as a gentle circuit.

Cloud step walk

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Step softly and lift the knee and hand together on the same side.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall with your feet together and your knees slightly bent. Soften your face and breathe smoothly.
Lift — Shift your weight onto your right foot. At the same time, lift your left knee up high and raise your left hand up beside your head.
Step — Lower your left foot to take a small step forward, your left hand sinking down as the foot touches the ground.
Shift — Move your weight onto your left foot, then lift your right knee high and your right hand up in the same way.
Repeat — Continue stepping softly — the arm and leg on the same side rising together — for the time given.

Heel-toe flow

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Place the heel gently and roll through the foot.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall, arms relaxed, weight settled evenly.
Step — Step one foot forward and place it down heel-first, toes lifted.
Roll — Slowly roll your weight through the foot — heel, ball, then toes — as the whole sole meets the floor.
Shift — Transfer your weight fully onto the front foot, keeping your head lifted and spine tall.
Repeat — Bring the back foot through and continue, walking slowly with short, measured steps.

Wave hands walk

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Lift your hands lightly in front and wave side to side as you step.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with soft knees. Raise one hand to chest height, palm toward you, and let the other sink low, palm up — as if softly holding a large ball.
Turn — Turn gently from the waist to one side, letting your hands trace slow circles past each other, like guiding clouds. Let the waist lead the arms.
Step — As you turn, take a soft step to the side, then bring the trailing foot in to meet it.
Breathe — Inhale as you turn one way, exhale as you turn back. Keep your hands no higher than eye level.
Repeat — Flow slowly from side to side, the waist always leading, for the time given.

Standing pause

30 seconds between rounds

Stand evenly on both feet and relax the arms.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with your feet under your hips, weight even on both feet.
Settle — Let your knees soften and your shoulders drop. Relax your arms and unclench your jaw.
Breathe — Take a few slow, easy breaths, feeling steady and rooted through both feet.
Rest — Simply stand and be still, letting the movement settle before the next round.
Small steps make the routine repeatable.
2

Balance Confidence

About 12 minutes · Chair or wall nearby · 3 slow rounds
Focus: Use support before you need it.  Move through the exercises below as a gentle circuit.

Lotus kick

6 each side × 3 rounds · Repeat both sides

Cup your hands in front and lift the knee up to meet them.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall and hold both hands out in front of you at about waist height, palms up and softly cupped.
Rotate — Lift one knee and let the hip turn gently, bringing the leg up in front of you.
Touch — Guide the knee or foot up toward your cupped hands, tapping lightly if you can reach — never straining for it.
Lower — Lower the leg with control, then repeat on the other side.
Keep it easy — Keep the movement low and slow. Height doesn't matter here — control does.

Side glide walk

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Step one foot to the side and bring the other in smoothly.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with your feet under your hips, weight even.
Step — Step one foot out to the side, letting your weight glide smoothly across.
Close — Bring the other foot in quietly to meet it, feet together.
Repeat — Continue gliding to the side with small, quiet steps.

Soft finish stance

30 seconds between rounds

Feet under hips, hands relaxed.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with your feet under your hips, weight even on both feet.
Settle — Let your knees soften and your shoulders drop. Relax your arms and unclench your jaw.
Breathe — Take a few slow, easy breaths, feeling steady and rooted through both feet.
Rest — Simply stand and be still, letting the movement settle before the next round.
Confidence grows from control.
3

Arms & Walking Flow

About 14 minutes · Open space · 3 slow rounds
Focus: Let the arms guide the rhythm, not the speed.  Move through the exercises below as a gentle circuit.

Brush knee step

8 each side × 3 rounds · Repeat both sides

Sweep one hand past the knee and step forward softly.

How to do it
Prepare — Settle your weight back and soften your hips.
Brush — As your weight begins to move forward, sweep your front hand down and gently past your knee, palm turning inward — a soft clearing, not a forceful sweep.
Push — Bring the other hand forward from near your waist, extending to about chest height, palm facing forward. Keep the elbow unlocked.
Shift — Pour your weight slowly onto the front foot, staying tall — don't lean into the push. Let the waist lead.
Repeat — Step through and repeat on the other side.

Forward push walk

8 slow steps × 3 rounds

Step forward small and press both palms without locking the elbows.

How to do it
Prepare — Take a small step forward, drawing both palms back near your chest.
Press — Slowly press both palms forward — as if moving gently through water — as your weight shifts onto the front foot.
Soften — Keep your elbows unlocked and your shoulders relaxed; move from the waist, not the arms.
Repeat — Step forward with small, even steps, pressing softly each time.

Silk reeling step

6 each side × 3 rounds · Repeat both sides

Circle the arms gently and shift your weight with the circle.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with soft knees, arms lightly forward.
Circle — Begin a slow, continuous circle with your arms, turning gently at the waist. Keep your wrists soft and the motion smooth.
Connect — Let your weight shift with the circle, your feet following the flow — hand, waist and foot moving as one.
Repeat — Keep circling slowly, one direction then the other, never breaking the flow.

Standing pause

30 seconds between rounds

Return to neutral and soften the knees.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with your feet under your hips, weight even on both feet.
Settle — Let your knees soften and your shoulders drop. Relax your arms and unclench your jaw.
Breathe — Take a few slow, easy breaths, feeling steady and rooted through both feet.
Rest — Simply stand and be still, letting the movement settle before the next round.
Smooth movement feels easier tomorrow.
4

Recovery & Mobility

About 10 minutes · Chair optional · 2 slow rounds
Focus: Make every movement comfortable.  Move through the exercises below as a gentle circuit.

Forward walk

60 seconds × 2 rounds

Walk with short strides and keep your head lifted.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall, head lifted, gaze forward.
Step — Walk forward with short, unhurried strides, placing each foot softly.
Flow — Let your arms swing naturally and breathe smoothly as you go.
Repeat — Keep the pace slow and even for the time given.

Shoulder wave

8 rounds × 2 rounds

Lift your hands softly and wave side to side.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall, arms relaxed, shoulders soft.
Lift — Raise both hands lightly to about chest height.
Wave — Gently wave your hands and shoulders from side to side, turning a little at the waist, keeping the movement loose and easy.
Repeat — Flow side to side, letting your shoulders roll softly with each wave.

Quiet stance

60 seconds between rounds

Stand evenly and relax the jaw.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with your feet under your hips, weight even on both feet.
Settle — Let your knees soften and your shoulders drop. Relax your arms and unclench your jaw.
Breathe — Take a few slow, easy breaths, feeling steady and rooted through both feet.
Rest — Simply stand and be still, letting the movement settle before the next round.
Recovery days keep the week moving.
5

Steady Strength

About 15 minutes · Chair or wall nearby · 3 slow rounds
Focus: Stay stacked over your feet.  Move through the exercises below as a gentle circuit.

Forward push walk

10 steps × 3 rounds

Step small and press the palms forward softly.

How to do it
Prepare — Take a small step forward, drawing both palms back near your chest.
Press — Slowly press both palms forward — as if moving gently through water — as your weight shifts onto the front foot.
Soften — Keep your elbows unlocked and your shoulders relaxed; move from the waist, not the arms.
Repeat — Step forward with small, even steps, pressing softly each time.

Side glide walk

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Step to the side and bring the feet together quietly.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with your feet under your hips, weight even.
Step — Step one foot out to the side, letting your weight glide smoothly across.
Close — Bring the other foot in quietly to meet it, feet together.
Repeat — Continue gliding to the side with small, quiet steps.

Heel-toe flow

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Place the heel first and roll smoothly.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall, arms relaxed, weight settled evenly.
Step — Step one foot forward and place it down heel-first, toes lifted.
Roll — Slowly roll your weight through the foot — heel, ball, then toes — as the whole sole meets the floor.
Shift — Transfer your weight fully onto the front foot, keeping your head lifted and spine tall.
Repeat — Bring the back foot through and continue, walking slowly with short, measured steps.
Balance is built one steady rep at a time.
6

Coordination Flow

About 16 minutes · Open space · 3 slow rounds
Focus: Connect foot, hand, and breath.  Move through the exercises below as a gentle circuit.

Cloud step walk

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Lift the knee and hand together, then step softly forward.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall with your feet together and your knees slightly bent. Soften your face and breathe smoothly.
Lift — Shift your weight onto your right foot. At the same time, lift your left knee up high and raise your left hand up beside your head.
Step — Lower your left foot to take a small step forward, your left hand sinking down as the foot touches the ground.
Shift — Move your weight onto your left foot, then lift your right knee high and your right hand up in the same way.
Repeat — Continue stepping softly — the arm and leg on the same side rising together — for the time given.

Brush knee step

8 each side × 3 rounds · Repeat both sides

Shift back slightly and sweep the hand past the knee.

How to do it
Prepare — Settle your weight back and soften your hips.
Brush — As your weight begins to move forward, sweep your front hand down and gently past your knee, palm turning inward — a soft clearing, not a forceful sweep.
Push — Bring the other hand forward from near your waist, extending to about chest height, palm facing forward. Keep the elbow unlocked.
Shift — Pour your weight slowly onto the front foot, staying tall — don't lean into the push. Let the waist lead.
Repeat — Step through and repeat on the other side.

Silk reeling step

8 circles × 3 rounds

Turn the torso gently and circle the arms with soft wrists.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with soft knees, arms lightly forward.
Circle — Begin a slow, continuous circle with your arms, turning gently at the waist. Keep your wrists soft and the motion smooth.
Connect — Let your weight shift with the circle, your feet following the flow — hand, waist and foot moving as one.
Repeat — Keep circling slowly, one direction then the other, never breaking the flow.

Wave hands walk

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Walk slowly and wave the hands side to side.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with soft knees. Raise one hand to chest height, palm toward you, and let the other sink low, palm up — as if softly holding a large ball.
Turn — Turn gently from the waist to one side, letting your hands trace slow circles past each other, like guiding clouds. Let the waist lead the arms.
Step — As you turn, take a soft step to the side, then bring the trailing foot in to meet it.
Breathe — Inhale as you turn one way, exhale as you turn back. Keep your hands no higher than eye level.
Repeat — Flow slowly from side to side, the waist always leading, for the time given.
Connected movement feels less effortful.
7

Repeatable Tai Chi Walk

About 18 minutes · Open space or chair nearby · 3 slow rounds
Focus: Use the movements you can repeat tomorrow.  Move through the exercises below as a gentle circuit.

Heel-toe flow

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Walk heel to toe and keep your steps measured.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall, arms relaxed, weight settled evenly.
Step — Step one foot forward and place it down heel-first, toes lifted.
Roll — Slowly roll your weight through the foot — heel, ball, then toes — as the whole sole meets the floor.
Shift — Transfer your weight fully onto the front foot, keeping your head lifted and spine tall.
Repeat — Bring the back foot through and continue, walking slowly with short, measured steps.

Cloud step walk

60 seconds × 3 rounds

Lift the knee and hand together, then step softly.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand tall with your feet together and your knees slightly bent. Soften your face and breathe smoothly.
Lift — Shift your weight onto your right foot. At the same time, lift your left knee up high and raise your left hand up beside your head.
Step — Lower your left foot to take a small step forward, your left hand sinking down as the foot touches the ground.
Shift — Move your weight onto your left foot, then lift your right knee high and your right hand up in the same way.
Repeat — Continue stepping softly — the arm and leg on the same side rising together — for the time given.

Forward push walk

10 steps × 3 rounds

Press the palms softly and keep the elbows unlocked.

How to do it
Prepare — Take a small step forward, drawing both palms back near your chest.
Press — Slowly press both palms forward — as if moving gently through water — as your weight shifts onto the front foot.
Soften — Keep your elbows unlocked and your shoulders relaxed; move from the waist, not the arms.
Repeat — Step forward with small, even steps, pressing softly each time.

Standing pause

30 seconds between rounds

Stand evenly and let everything settle.

How to do it
Prepare — Stand with your feet under your hips, weight even on both feet.
Settle — Let your knees soften and your shoulders drop. Relax your arms and unclench your jaw.
Breathe — Take a few slow, easy breaths, feeling steady and rooted through both feet.
Rest — Simply stand and be still, letting the movement settle before the next round.
This is your go-to 7-day finish flow.
✦

Keep going, gently

Repeat your favourite day, restart the week, or use one move as a short daily reset.

Day 2☐

Day 6☐
Day 7☐
Set the spaceChoose a clear walking area with room to turn around comfortably.
Choose controlShorter steps count. Smooth and steady is better than fast or forced.
Use one cuePick one reliable time of day so the routine becomes easier to remember.
Your next step: repeat your favourite day, restart the 7-day plan, or choose one movement as a short daily reset. Little and often always wins.
